description: conformance fixture: tri-state specVersion (0.1.0/0.2.0/0.3.0) cross-version pairs. case-1..4 are encoded centrally, avoiding silent skips caused by scattering them across the cross_version arrays embedded in individual fixtures. Each sample pair declares a schemaId + input + expected result (valid: true/false), driven uniformly by loadFixtureDir.

cases.description: case-1: a v0.1.0 envelope shape (no capabilityTokenRef, no v0.3.0 extension fields) fed to the unified validator -> accept (forward compatible: an older-version wire must retain accept semantics under a newer-version validator; tri-state coexistence)

case-2: a v0.3.0 envelope carries capabilityTokenRef but the specVersion is mislabeled as 0.3.0 -> if/then branch rejects (capabilityTokenRef is only legal on a 0.2.0 envelope; this pair serves as an executable projection of 'cross-version field misuse': an older-version validator does not recognize the new field, and a newer-version validator must reject cross-branch misuse per the version gate, with the same semantics as 'v0.3.0 envelope on v0.1.0 validator -> reject specVersion mismatch')
cases.expectedError: specVersion must be 0.2.0 when header.capabilityTokenRef is present

cases.description: case-3: a v0.3.0 ActionRecord contains the SESSION_SUPERSEDED action but the specVersion is mislabeled as 0.1.0 -> the SESSION_SUPERSEDED branch in schemas.ts forces specVersion=0.3.0 (fail-closed). Equivalent to '0.3.0 record rejected on 0.1.0 validator': the 0.1.0 enum does not include SESSION_SUPERSEDED
case-4: a v0.3.0 ActionRecord missing delegationDepth -> the allOf v0.3.0 branch in schemas.ts rejects via required (v0.3.0 promotes delegationDepth to required)
